creator: lapo@lapo.it
text:
Boris Samorodov wrote:
> It's OK at my tinderbox. Can you get a clue how to reproduce it?

It's probably a side-effect of the fact that porttools build in /tmp/XXX
instead of the usual directory, maybe (but I thought that tinderbox did
similar similar trick too, or isn't that so?).

> Hm, I don't have warnings:
> -----
> % portlint -aC

Err, what's happening? Same portlint, different results.

% portlint -aC
WARN: Makefile: "BUILD_DEPENDS" has to appear earlier.
WARN: Makefile: "RUN_DEPENDS" has to appear earlier.
FATAL: /home/lapo/ports/qct/distinfo: [1]: unsupported checksum
algorithm found: MD5.
WARN: no CVS directories. Use -N to check a new port.
1 fatal error and 3 warnings found.
% pkg_info -Ix portlint
portlint-2.13.5     A verifier for FreeBSD port directory
% uname -a
FreeBSD lapo.andxor.it 8.2-RELEASE FreeBSD 8.2-RELEASE #0: [...] amd64

Another important thing: latest port always fails with:

% qct
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/qct", line 5, in <module>
    pkg_resources.run_script('qct==1.7', 'qct')
  File "build/bdist.freebsd-8.2-RELEASE-amd64/egg/pkg_resources.py",
line 489, in run_script
  File "build/bdist.freebsd-8.2-RELEASE-amd64/egg/pkg_resources.py",
line 1207, in run_script
  File
"/usr/local/lib/python2.6/site-packages/qct-1.7-py2.6.egg/EGG-INFO/scripts/qct",
line 13, in <module>
    from qctlib.gui_logic import CommitTool
  File
"/usr/local/lib/python2.6/site-packages/qct-1.7-py2.6.egg/qctlib/gui_logic.py",
line 9, in <module>
    from qctlib.ui_dialog import Ui_commitToolDialog
ImportError: No module named ui_dialog

I was meaning to check that better before reporting (and maybe have a
patch ready), but as far as I understand of Python (i.e. very little!)
it's searching for a ui_dialog.py file which is missing in the package,
but also in the very same source tarball (!!!) that was working in the
1.7_4 version of the package. I don't know if that problem is mine or
what, but I wrote it just in case; if you don't get that, just ignore
this part of the message, I'll check better in the next days (and erport
back if needed).
